signals.what: SWA1846 squawking 7600 (radio failure), 3 observation(s), single network.
signals.narrative: Radio failure squawk from a Southwest 737 inbound to Phoenix; no distress pattern in track. Likely avionics fault.

signals.what: 2 aircraft squawking 7700 within 500 km of each other inside 30 min — not a routine diversion pattern.
signals.narrative: Two unrelated medical diversions; both aircraft landed normally per track data.

signals.what: EJA520 squawking 7500 (unlawful interference), 2 observation(s), single network — below the corroborated-S1 bar, watching.
signals.narrative: Fractional bizjet over the Gulf; 7500 held two polls then cleared. Consistent with transponder mis-set.

briefing.headline: ORRERY — Night Watch Briefing, 20 Jul 2026
briefing.open: Coverage nominal. One radio-failure squawk near home region worth a glance; Baltic corridor remains noisy but consistent with its baseline.
